stories.title: Phillies Make History — Three Consecutive Go-Ahead 9th-Inning Home Runs
stories.angle: The Phillies became the first team in MLB history to hit a go-ahead home run in the ninth inning of three consecutive games. Kyle Schwarber, who leads MLB with 29 home runs, is central to the surge. The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home field — and the streak adds urgency to his Derby participation question.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Phillies are the first team in MLB history to hit a go-ahead home run in the ninth inning of three consecutive games.

Three straight nights. Three ninth-inning leads taken.

Schwarber has 29 HRs. The Derby is at Citizens Bank Park. This team is on something. #Phillies

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 29 HRs, MLB lead.

His team: the first in history to hit go-ahead 9th-inning HRs in three straight games.

The Home Run Derby is at his home park on July 13. He still hasn't committed to entering.

Someone should call him. #Phillies

facebook.long_form:
The Phillies just did something that has never been done in MLB history.

Three consecutive games. Three go-ahead home runs in the ninth inning. Not home runs that tied the game or added insurance runs. Home runs that gave the Phillies the lead — in the ninth — in three straight games.

That is a streak without precedent in the history of the sport.

The timing matters. Kyle Schwarber, who leads all of baseball with 29 home runs, is central to the surge. He had three home runs in a single game before driving in the team during this ninth-inning stretch. The Home Run Derby is scheduled for July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home field, in front of a Philadelphia crowd that would lose its mind if he entered. He still hasn't committed to participating.

The Phillies play a brand of baseball that would wear down a pitcher over 162 games. They are not a team that is going to beat you in the first inning and coast. They are a team that is going to make you throw your best stuff in the eighth and ninth and then make you regret it.

Three consecutive ninth-inning go-ahead home runs. That is the team the rest of the NL East is dealing with right now.

#Phillies #MLB #Baseball
[Posting Window: 8:00 AM ET]

stories.title: All-Star Voting Phase 2 — Two Days Away, Ohtani and Clement Locked In
stories.angle: Phase 2 opens June 29, closes July 2 at noon ET. Ohtani (3,341,257 Phase 1 votes) earns an automatic DH start in the NL. Ernie Clement (3,232,932 Phase 1 votes) locks in as the automatic AL starter. The Phase 2 voting format resets counters and features the top two finalists at each position. All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
All-Star Voting Phase 2 opens Sunday.

Ohtani (3.34M Phase 1 votes) is locked in as the automatic NL DH starter.
Ernie Clement is the automatic AL starter.

Phase 2 resets the counters. Two days to go. Game is July 14 in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Phase 2 All-Star voting: June 29–July 2.

The players who dominated Phase 1 still have to hold off Phase 2 challengers.

Ohtani and Clement are locked in. Everyone else is still competing.

July 14. Citizens Bank Park. Philadelphia. #MLBAllStar

facebook.long_form:
Phase 1 of All-Star voting ended on June 25. Phase 2 opens Sunday, June 29.

Two automatic starters are already decided. Shohei Ohtani received 3,341,257 Phase 1 votes — the most of any player in baseball, NL or AL — and earns an automatic DH starting assignment for the National League. Ernie Clement received 3,232,932 Phase 1 votes in the American League, locking in his automatic start.

Phase 2 changes the format. The top two vote-getters at each position advance to Phase 2, and the counters reset to zero. The players who dominated Phase 1 by wide margins will likely extend their leads, but the format gives challengers a legitimate path to overturn a close Phase 1 race.

Voting runs June 29 through July 2 at noon ET. Starters are announced shortly after. The All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia — the same park hosting the Home Run Derby the night before.

The 2026 All-Star week in Philadelphia is already shaping up as one of the louder settings in recent years. A building that makes noise for every Phillies home game does not suddenly go quiet for an exhibition.

#MLB #AllStarGame #Baseball
[Posting Window: 10:30 AM ET]

stories.title: Bobby Witt Jr. — Back in the Lineup, Eyes on the All-Star Game
stories.angle: Witt Jr. is trending toward a return after a Grade 1 MCL sprain sidelined him for six games. Manager Matt Quatraro called the recovery "very encouraging." With Witt leading AL fWAR at 4.5 entering June, his return resets the Royals' second-half picture and his All-Star Game case for the July 14 game in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Bobby Witt Jr. is expected back in the lineup after a Grade 1 MCL sprain kept him out 6 games.

He leads the AL with 4.5 fWAR.

The All-Star Game is July 14 in Philadelphia. He has 17 days to make sure he's there. #Royals

facebook.long_form:
Bobby Witt Jr. is expected to return to the Royals' lineup after missing six games with a Grade 1 MCL sprain.

The injury happened on June 18, when Witt slid awkwardly while fielding and felt immediate discomfort. A Grade 1 sprain is the least severe classification — ligament stretched but not torn. The Royals avoided placing him on the injured list, which is itself a signal of how confident they were in a quick recovery.

Manager Matt Quatraro described Witt's preparation as "very encouraging." He ran the bases, completed defensive drills, and hit in the batting cage — the checklist a player completes before being cleared to play.

What makes Witt's return significant beyond the box score is where his season stands. He was leading the American League in fWAR at 4.5 before the injury. That number is not just a statistic. It is the clearest single-number argument for why Witt should be starting at shortstop at Citizens Bank Park on July 14.

The All-Star Game is 17 days away. Witt has time to make the final case that his first half — before and after the six-game absence — is the best in the AL.

#Royals #BobbyWitt #Baseball
[Posting Window: 12:30 PM ET]

stories.title: Dansby Swanson's 15-RBI Series — The Stat That Puts His Season in Context
stories.angle: Dansby Swanson totaled 15 RBIs in a four-game series against the Mets, one of the rarest offensive series in the modern era. The performance puts his 2026 season in a different frame — not just a contributing player on the Braves, but a legitimate MVP-adjacent contributor on a team that entered June 27 with the best record in the NL East at 48-31.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Dansby Swanson drove in 15 runs in a four-game series against the Mets.

Fifteen.

The Braves are 48-31, best record in the NL East. Swanson is the reason that number is real. #Braves

facebook.long_form:
Dansby Swanson drove in 15 runs in a four-game series against the Mets.

That is not a number that happens very often. A four-game series is a finite opportunity — maybe 20 plate appearances, maybe fewer. Accumulating 15 RBIs in that window requires the right combination of timing, lineup support, and situational hitting. Swanson produced all three against the Mets.

The production puts his 2026 season in a different frame. Swanson has been a solid, reliable contributor for Atlanta throughout his career. But a 15-RBI series is not solid and reliable. It is dominant.

The Braves are 48-31, the best record in the NL East, and they are doing it without Acuña — who missed significant time earlier in the season. The team's ability to find production from players like Swanson and Ozzie Albies in the middle of the lineup is what makes them a credible postseason threat rather than a wild card afterthought.

Swanson's name rarely shows up in the MVP conversation. After a four-game, 15-RBI performance against the division's top competition, it should.

#Braves #DansbySwanson #MLB
[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

stories.title: 37 Days to the Trade Deadline — Skubal and the Market Taking Shape
stories.angle: August 3 is 37 days away. Tarik Skubal has an estimated 85 percent trade probability based on the Tigers' standing and his impending free agency. Four contenders have emerged: Dodgers, Padres, Blue Jays, Yankees. Aroldis Chapman has 28 consecutive saves, one shy of his career record, and is the best reliever available if the Red Sox decide to sell.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
37 days to the trade deadline.

Tarik Skubal: 2x Cy Young. Won't extend. Tigers in last place. Estimated 85% trade probability.

Four teams are already circling: Dodgers, Padres, Blue Jays, Yankees.

August 3 is coming fast.

facebook.long_form:
August 3 is 37 days away. The trade deadline market is already forming.

Tarik Skubal is the centerpiece. Two Cy Young Awards. A fastball that is back at 99.9 mph after returning from midseason bone chip surgery. A Tigers team sitting in last place and carrying no realistic path to a second-half collapse of the deficit between them and contention. Skubal will not extend his contract with Detroit. He is a rental, and every team that knows what good pitching costs at the deadline is already doing the math.

An estimated 85 percent trade probability is circulating around Skubal from credible outlets. Four teams have emerged as the early suitors: the Dodgers, the Padres, the Blue Jays, and the Yankees. If that list narrows to a two-team race, the bidding for one good pitching package — a top prospect plus multiple pieces — will get expensive quickly.

Aroldis Chapman adds a different dynamic to the market. He has converted 28 consecutive saves — one shy of his career record — since rediscovering his elite form in Boston. If the Red Sox decide to sell, Chapman becomes the best closer available, with the durability and the contract that makes him worth pursuing.

Thirty-seven days is a long time in baseball. Teams that are buyers in late June sometimes become sellers by mid-July. But the Skubal market is going to be one of the louder storylines between now and August 3.

#MLB #TradeDeadline #TarikSkubal
[Posting Window: 6:00 PM ET]
